mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) raises inflation risk premiums and increases the probability of further monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates recession probabilities, tightens financial conditions, and reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) indicate a labor-hoarding divergence where strong employment sustains wage costs while output declines, squeezing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146135.0 and INDPRO at 105.0284 as of Nov 2022
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increasing and INDPRO decreasing fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, stronger retail sales (RSAFS) prompt markets to reprice the terminal federal funds rate higher, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns follow month-over-month RSAFS increases in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity, falling capacity utilization, and declining corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months when INDPRO 3-month change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) that rises more than 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor demand, which reduces household income growth and expands equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP levels (GDPC1) reflect stronger aggregate income and corporate profit generation, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between GDPC1 levels and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of declining industrial production (INDPRO) and elevated consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) captures a stagflationary environment where supply constraints raise costs while output falls, jointly eroding earnings and forcing valuation multiple compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 243.846 and INDPRO at 105.0284 as of Nov 2022
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 20-day S&P 500 returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in a walk-forward regression.
